evidence: holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `goseg/shipworkflow/` has 44 files and mixed concern clusters (newship, startram, urbit, workflow, lifecycle) in one flat folder, exceeding the >10 mixed-concern split signal.

`goseg/system/disk_fstab.go` and `goseg/system/disk_health.go` are parent-package wrappers that primarily delegate to subpackages (`groundseg/system/disk` and `groundseg/system/storage`), indicating placement outside their implementation affinity.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `goseg/config/` has 41 files; `bootstrap.go` (process startup/env/path init) and `config.go` (runtime context/state snapshots/settings types) coexist with many unrelated config domains in one directory.

Runtime seam/default wiring is repeated manually across modules: `normalizeAccessPointRuntime` in `goseg/accesspoint/accesspoint.go`, `NewWiFiRuntime` + `NewWiFiRuntimeWith` in `goseg/system/wifi_runtime_methods.go`, and many `runtime*Ops` constructor functions in `goseg/docker/orchestration/runtime_ops.go`.

`goseg/protocol/actions/actions.go` combines contract bootstrapping, namespace lookup APIs, compatibility checks, and payload marshaling in one file, while `goseg/protocol/actions/actions_test.go` has to validate all of these concerns together.

Several test files build bespoke seam/runtime DSLs instead of reusing one test runtime pattern (`goseg/importer/importer_test.go`, `goseg/startuporchestrator/startup_orchestrator_test.go`, `goseg/system/wifi_test.go`, `goseg/accesspoint/accesspoint_test.go`).

The same Wi-Fi subsystem mixes `WiFi` and `Wifi` in exported and internal names (`NewWiFiRuntime`, `isWiFiRadioEnabled`, `ErrWiFiInterfaceNotFound` vs `NewWifiClient`, `WifiInfoTicker`, `testWifiRuntime`).
Config naming mixes abbreviated and full forms in public and core patch types (`Conf()`, `ConfPatch`, `ConfUpdateOption`) alongside `Config()`, `ConfigPatch`, and `ConfigUpdateOption` aliases.
Session logstream naming drifts between `Sys*` and `System*` for the same concept (`SysLogSessions`, `SysSessionsToRemove` vs `SystemLogMessages`, `PublishSystemLog`).

findings.summary: The `shipworkflow` directory is a flat mixed-concern package, obscuring ownership boundaries between lifecycle/newship/startram/urbit flows.
evidence: directory_profiles reports 44 files under `goseg/shipworkflow/`, with filenames spanning multiple domains (`newship_*`, `startram_*`, `urbit_*`, `workflow_*`, `lifecycle.go`) in one folder.

`doc.go` describes broad coordination responsibilities while `lifecycle.go` is a narrow lifecycle utility, showing fine-grained and broad orchestration artifacts co-located without subpackage boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create target folders `goseg/shipworkflow/lifecycle`, `goseg/shipworkflow/newship`, `goseg/shipworkflow/startram`, `goseg/shipworkflow/urbit`; (2) move lowest-dependency files first (`lifecycle.go` + tests), then move `newship_*`, then `startram_*`, then `urbit_*`; (3) keep a temporary compatibility facade in `shipworkflow` that forwards to subpackages, then remove once callers migrate; (4) update imports with `rg 'groundseg/shipworkflow' goseg -n` and package refs via `gofmt -w goseg/shipworkflow/**/*.go`; (5) validate with `go test ./goseg/shipworkflow/...` and `go test ./...`.

findings.summary: Disk/fstab APIs are placed in parent `system` as thin adapters even though implementation affinity is in `system/disk` and `system/storage`.
evidence: `disk_fstab.go` only aliases `systemdisk.FstabRecord` and forwards parse/reconcile/read/write calls directly to `groundseg/system/disk`.
`disk_health.go` forwards SMART and disk listing operations directly to `groundseg/system/storage` and seam resolution, adding no parent-level orchestration policy.
`disk_fstab_test.go` is a placeholder smoke test, so wrapper placement remains unvalidated as a deliberate API boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) move/define these entry points under `goseg/system/disk` as canonical API (`fstab.go`, `health.go`), keeping parent `system` wrappers only as deprecated shims; (2) migrate direct consumers to `groundseg/system/disk` imports in batches (read-only callers first, mutating callers second); (3) remove shims after one release window; (4) update imports via `rg 'groundseg/system"|groundseg/system$' goseg -n` and `gofmt -w goseg/system/**/*.go`; (5) validate with `go test ./goseg/system/... ./goseg/system/disk/... ./goseg/system/storage/...`.

findings.summary: Process bootstrap concerns and runtime-context state modeling are co-located in `config`, increasing directory boundary blur in an already large package.
evidence: `bootstrap.go` handles startup sequencing (`Initialize`), env/argv probing (`getBasePath`, `setDebugMode`), and persistence bootstrapping.
`config.go` defines broad settings snapshots plus mutable runtime context accessors (`RuntimeContextSnapshot`, `SetBasePath`, `SetDebugMode`) alongside config state models.
directory_profiles reports `goseg/config/` has 41 files, indicating a high-load folder where mixed startup/runtime/config-domain concerns reduce navigability.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: (1) create `goseg/config/runtime` for runtime context/env/arch/debug path concerns and `goseg/config/bootstrap` for init orchestration; (2) move pure runtime-context functions/types first (`RuntimeContext*`, `Set*` runtime setters), then move init flow (`Initialize`, path/session/bootstrap helpers); (3) keep `config` package as stable facade exposing selected APIs during migration; (4) update imports with `rg 'groundseg/config' goseg -n` and run `gofmt -w goseg/config/**/*.go`; (5) validate with `go test ./goseg/config/...` and `go test ./...`.

findings.summary: The `protocol/contracts` directory is a flat mixed-concern package that combines domain-specific families and shared contract machinery.
evidence: `protocol_contracts.go` defines websocket action-family IDs and bindings, while `startram_contracts.go` defines StarTram API error contracts with different governance scope.

Packet structure profile reports 11 files in `goseg/protocol/contracts/`, crossing the flat-directory overload threshold with mixed responsibilities (family-specific catalogs + shared validators/tests).
`family_specs_test.go` validates shared family-builder behavior in the same folder as concrete family definitions, signaling missing subpackage boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: create `goseg/protocol/contracts/shared/` for reusable catalog/family types and validators, `goseg/protocol/contracts/families/protocol/` for websocket action contracts, and `goseg/protocol/contracts/families/startram/` for StarTram contracts. Stage 2: move shared files first (`family_specs*`, shared builders/metadata), then move `protocol_contracts*`, then `startram_contracts*`. Stage 3: update imports with `rg "groundseg/protocol/contracts" goseg/protocol -n` and `goimports -w ./goseg/protocol/contracts/...`. Stage 4: validate with `go test ./goseg/protocol/contracts/... ./goseg/protocol/conformance/...` and then `go test ./...`.

findings.summary: Protocol action identity ownership is split across `actionids`, `actions`, and `contracts`, creating weak package ownership boundaries for compatibility-critical constants.
evidence: `actionids.go` declares canonical namespace/action strings.
`protocol_contracts.go` imports `actionids` and creates parallel typed aliases/constants for the same action tokens.
`fixtures_test.go` validates fixtures against `protocol/actions` constants instead of a single contracts-owned source, showing cross-package contract coupling.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: establish a single ownership package, e.g. `goseg/protocol/contracts/bindings/` (or `goseg/protocol/actions/bindings/`) as the sole canonical namespace/action registry used by actions, contracts, and conformance. Stage 2: move `actionids.go` into that target package and migrate `protocol_contracts.go`/fixtures consumers to import only that API. Stage 3: remove duplicate constant re-exports after migration. Stage 4: run `rg "NamespaceUpload|UploadOpenEndpoint|C2CConnect" goseg/protocol -n` to update references, then `go test ./goseg/protocol/actions ./goseg/protocol/contracts ./goseg/protocol/conformance`.

findings.summary: Governance and quality-gate docs point to broad policy, but runtime contract ownership lacks a single package entrypoint aligned with those docs.
evidence: `README.md` references governance and backend quality-gate policy files but does not map those policies to a clear runtime contract package boundary.
Concrete contract families are split within a shared `contracts` package without an obvious top-level API surface for compatibility policy enforcement.
This docs-to-runtime mapping gap raises navigation and change-risk for contract governance updates.
findings.suggestion: Stage 1: introduce `goseg/protocol/contracts/catalog/` (or `api/`) as the documented entrypoint package exposing only stable discovery/validation interfaces. Stage 2: relocate family implementations under `families/*` and keep policy-facing APIs in `catalog`. Stage 3: update `README.md` governance section to reference the new package layout and ownership map. Stage 4: validate with `go test ./goseg/protocol/contracts/...` and a docs link check via `rg "protocol/contracts" README.md docs/architecture -n`.

findings.summary: Runtime default/override wiring is duplicated across subsystems with inconsistent composition rules.
evidence: `accesspoint.go` duplicates baseline config assignment in both `defaultAccessPointContext` and `accessPointRuntime`, then repeats field-by-field fallback logic in `normalizeAccessPointRuntime`.
`wifi_runtime_methods.go` needs special repair logic in `NewWiFiRuntimeWith` (`if overrides.RunNmcliFn == nil { ... }`) because seam merge behavior is not uniform.
`runtime_ops.go` has many near-boilerplate `runtimeContextOps/runtimeFileOps/runtimeImageOps/...` constructors that manually wire package functions into seam structs.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one shared seam-construction pattern (e.g., `defaults + override merge + validate`) and apply it across accesspoint, wifi, and orchestration runtimes. Keep defaults in a single source per runtime and eliminate duplicate default constructors/normalizers.

findings.summary: The actions module mixes registry lifecycle, API querying, and transport payload concerns in one unit.
evidence: `actions.go` contains init-time contract loading (`ensureActionContractsInitialized/initializeActionContracts`), namespace/action query APIs, lifecycle compatibility helpers, and C2C payload serialization (`NewC2CPayload/MarshalC2CPayload`).
`actions_test.go` correspondingly spans parsing, metadata, compatibility boundaries, and payload marshaling in one test surface, indicating one module is carrying multiple responsibilities.
Contract-policy tests in `contracts_test.go` and action-policy tests in `actions_test.go` overlap conceptually, increasing cross-module coupling around contract lifecycle behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split `actions.go` into focused units: contract registry/cache, namespace query API, and payload codec. Keep compatibility checks close to the registry layer and expose a smaller facade for callers.

findings.summary: The disk adapter adds pass-through indirection without owning behavior or migration boundaries.
evidence: `adapter.go` is almost entirely thin wrappers to `storage` functions (`ParseFstabLine`, `ReconcileFstabLines`, `ReadFstabLines`, `CreateGroundSegFilesystem`, etc.), often injecting `storage.DefaultSeams()` inline.
`storage_test.go` validates the storage behavior directly, which implies the adapter layer contributes little behavioral boundary while still creating an extra API surface to maintain.
findings.suggestion: Either complete the migration by moving callers to `system/storage` and removing adapter wrappers, or define a real boundary (interface + explicit seams owned by `system/disk`) so the adapter adds stable domain behavior rather than simple forwarding.

findings.summary: Test seam setup is reimplemented per package, creating drift-prone and incomplete runtime builders.
evidence: `importer_test.go` defines multiple near-identical runtime guard helpers (`initRuntime`, `uploadRuntime`, `provisionRuntime`) and custom runtime bootstrap helpers.
`startup_orchestrator_test.go` maintains a large manual callback-setter map (`startBackgroundServiceCallbackSetters`) to inject runtime seams.

`wifi_test.go` builds a partial copier in `testWifiRuntime` that copies only selected fields from `wifiRuntime` and omits others (e.g., `RunNmcliFn`, `WifiInfoTicker`), increasing risk that tests diverge from production runtime behavior.
`accesspoint_test.go` relies on global seam restoration utilities and package-level state mutation patterns distinct from other test suites.
findings.suggestion: Create one shared test seam builder utility for runtime structs (defaults + typed overrides + validation of missing callbacks) and migrate package tests to that pattern; remove duplicated guard helpers and manual setter maps.

findings.summary: Wi-Fi symbols use inconsistent acronym casing (`WiFi` vs `Wifi`) within one subsystem.
evidence: `goseg/system/wifi_runtime_methods.go` defines `NewWiFiRuntime`, `isWiFiRadioEnabled`, and `ErrWiFiInterfaceNotFound` usage, but the same runtime struct exposes `NewWifiClient` and `WifiInfoTicker`.
`goseg/system/wifi_test.go` mirrors the same drift with helpers like `resetWifiSeamsForTest`, `testWifiRuntime`, plus tests for `StartWiFiInfoLoop` and `RefreshWiFiInfo`.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical acronym style for this package (prefer Go initialism style, e.g. `WiFi`) and rename fields/helpers consistently (`NewWiFiClient`, `WiFiInfoTicker`, `resetWiFiSeamsForTest`, `testWiFiRuntime`) with compatibility shims only where required.

findings.summary: Config APIs split between `Conf` and `Config`, creating avoidable vocabulary drift.
evidence: `goseg/config/bootstrap.go` exports both `Config()` and `Conf()` (documented as backward-compatible alias), leaving two names for the same concept.
`goseg/config/config_update_schema.go` defines `ConfPatch`/`ConfUpdateOption` and then aliases `ConfigPatch`/`ConfigUpdateOption`, duplicating terminology in core schema types.
`goseg/config/config.go` uses full-form `Config` naming heavily (`SetStartramConfig`, `GetStartramConfigSnapshot`), increasing inconsistency with `Conf*` symbols.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on `Config` for public/core names and deprecate `Conf*` aliases behind transitional wrappers; update call sites and tests so one term is canonical in code search and API docs.

findings.summary: Logstream session store mixes `Sys` and `System` prefixes for the same domain concept.
evidence: `goseg/session/logstream_session_store.go` interface and methods use both `SysLogSessions`/`SysSessionsToRemove` and `SystemLogMessages`/`PublishSystemLog` for one system-log responsibility.
`goseg/logger/logger_test.go` uses explicit `SystemLog` terminology (`testSystemLogSink`, `drainSystemLogChannel`), reinforcing that session store abbreviations are a local outlier.
findings.suggestion: Normalize session-store method names to one vocabulary (prefer `System*` for clarity), e.g. `SystemLogSessions`, `SystemSessionsToRemove`, with temporary forwarding methods for backwards compatibility if needed.
